content: We can reframe Self-Refine's single-perspective iterative improvement framework by incorporating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives at the core of LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that iteratively refines outputs across competing inconsistent viewpoints rather than just refining a single initial output. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data while leveraging the perspective diversity of modern self-reflection mechanisms to catch blind spots a single self-feedback loop would miss.
